title: ws: GROUP_LEAVE uses pool.begin() (DEFERRED) not BEGIN IMMEDIATE — TOCTOU on is_group_member check
description: GROUP_LEAVE handler (ws.rs line 1707) calls is_group_member() and remove_member_and_maybe_delete_group() as two separate store operations with no shared transaction. is_group_member() reads inside pool.begin() (DEFERRED), then the outer code proceeds to remove_member_and_maybe_delete_group(). Between the two calls a concurrent GROUP_ADD from another client can add the same user back. More critically, the group existence check at line 1736 (get_group) is also a separate transaction from the subsequent is_group_member check. This creates a two-step TOCTOU: group exists + caller is member → remove. A concurrent GROUP_LEAVE from another session of the same pub_id can remove the user between the membership check and the remove call, causing remove_member_and_maybe_delete_group to silently no-op (the DELETE is idempotent) but still return Ok with success. This is low-severity but inconsistent with the documented atomicity claim. Fix: add group existence check inside remove_member_and_maybe_delete_group and return a meaningful result (was-member bool) rather than relying on the caller to pre-check membership.
